projects.projects-title.title: Applied Architecture
projects.projects-title.subtitle: Systems designed, automated and operated in real environments
projects.projects-title.body: In this section I present projects where I apply systems thinking in real environments: infrastructure design, automation and evolving services. Each project is presented as a complete system, not as an isolated proof of concept.
projects.homelab.title: On-Premise Autonomous Infrastructure
projects.homelab.subtitle: Platform designed to operate digital services with full control over network, power and deployment
projects.homelab.toptext: I design and maintain my own infrastructure where I experiment with networks, containers, reverse proxy, VLAN segmentation and progressive automation. It's not just a lab: it's a real environment that hosts productive services and allows me to practice end-to-end architecture, deployment and observability.
projects.homelab.bullets: Network segmentation with VLAN (LAN, DMX, IoT, Energy, Guest)
Reverse proxy Nginx and Docker services on internal network
Monitoring and analysis of traffic and performance
Design prepared for evolution to IaC (Terraform + Ansible)
projects.homelab.casestudy.overview: My homelab is a production-grade on-premise infrastructure I design, deploy and maintain myself. It hosts real services including this website, a newsletter service, and internal tooling — all running on physical hardware with full network control.
projects.homelab.casestudy.challenge: Running personal digital services at home with the reliability, security and observability of a professional environment. The challenge was not just technical but architectural: designing a layered system that could grow incrementally without losing control.
projects.homelab.casestudy.solution: I implemented a layered architecture using a UniFi Dream Machine SE as the gateway with VLAN segmentation for traffic isolation. All services run in Docker containers behind an Nginx reverse proxy. A separate IoT/Energy VLAN isolates smart home devices from the main network.
projects.homelab.casestudy.tech_list: UniFi Dream Machine SE — gateway, firewall, VLAN management
Nginx — reverse proxy with SSL termination
Tesla Powerwall & Solax — energy management over IoT VLAN
Terraform + Ansible (in progress) — infrastructure as code
projects.homelab.casestudy.outcome: A stable, self-managed infrastructure running 24/7 with isolated VLANs, automated deployments and real-time monitoring. It serves as both a production environment for my personal services and a continuous learning platform for advanced infrastructure topics.
projects.personalwebsite.title: Personal Website - Architecture as a product
projects.personalwebsite.subtitle: Public web designed as an evolving system, not as a static template
projects.personalwebsite.toptext: This website is not just a professional presentation. It's an environment where I apply component-based architecture, version control and continuous deployment. It's designed to evolve towards its own backend, microservices and automation.
projects.personalwebsite.bullets: Spring Boot backend and decoupled services
projects.personalwebsite.casestudy.overv: This website is an evolving engineering project, not a static portfolio. It demonstrates how I approach software architecture: starting with a clean component structure, adding automation, and designing for future extensibility.
projects.personalwebsite.casestudy.chall: Building a professional personal website that genuinely showcases engineering skills — not just aesthetically, but structurally. The challenge was to design a system that could grow from a simple frontend to a full-stack platform with backend services and automation.
projects.personalwebsite.casestudy.solut: I chose React + Vite for a fast, modular frontend, containerised with Docker and deployed automatically via GitHub Actions. The architecture separates UI primitives, domain components, and pages in a scalable folder structure. i18n is integrated from the start to support multiple languages.
projects.personalwebsite.casestudy.tech_: Docker + Nginx — containerisation and reverse proxy
GitHub Actions — CI/CD pipeline (build, test, deploy)
projects.personalwebsite.casestudy.outco: A continuously deployed, containerised website with a clean component architecture. Each deployment is automated through GitHub Actions, and the system is designed to extend with backend APIs and new features without restructuring the codebase.
projects.weaver.title: Weaver - Decentralized community platform
projects.weaver.subtitle: Architecture for physical communities with certified identity and cooperative structure
projects.weaver.toptext: Weaver is a project in development that explores how to design a social network based on real physical communities. The challenge is not only functional, but architectural: identity, permissions, scalability, backend and controlled deployment.
projects.weaver.bullets: Modular architecture with separation of services
Containerization and deployment with Docker (future K8s)
Design oriented to permissions and segmented communities
projects.weaver.casestudy.overview: Weaver is a platform designed to serve real physical communities — neighborhoods, co-ops, local associations — rather than algorithmically-driven online groups. The project explores how to combine verified identity, cooperative governance and scalable architecture.
projects.weaver.casestudy.challenge: Designing a social network where membership is tied to physical reality (address verification, community membership) instead of self-reported profiles. The architectural challenge involves building a permission system that reflects real community structures, not just follower graphs.
projects.weaver.casestudy.solution: A modular microservices architecture where each domain — users, auth, communities, permissions — is a separate service. The frontend is a mobile app that communicates through a unified API layer. Identity verification and community logic are decoupled to allow different verification strategies per deployment.
projects.weaver.casestudy.tech_list: JWT + custom auth — identity and permission management
API Gateway — unified entry point for all services
projects.weaver.casestudy.outcome: An MVP architecture currently in development with a clear service boundary design, a permission model based on community membership, and a deployment strategy that scales horizontally. The project is being built incrementally with production-readiness as the target.
